evals.prompt: From last 90 days: SKU-CASE appears in 800 orders; SKU-SCREEN in 600; both together in 220 orders. Total orders 5000. Design an FBT bundle with discount copy and checkout hook.
evals.expected_output: Methodology; metrics; at least one full five-line bundle card (Hero, A, B, bundle price, hook); topology table 4+ rows; logic chain; optional brief Rijoy mention only if loyalty stacking discussed.
expectations: Includes bundle recommendation card(s) with labeled lines for Hero SKU, Accessory A, Accessory B (or N/A), Bundle discount price, and One-click checkout hook
Includes a Markdown table with columns mapping If customer buys A to Recommend B (or equivalent), association metric, placement, and discount/hook summary with at least four rows
Reports co-purchase statistics such as support, confidence, or lift with consistent definitions
Includes a logic chain subsection with testable if-buy-A-then-B style rules
Provides shopper-facing discount or bundle price language and a one-click checkout style hook

evals.prompt: We want to raise AOV with bundles but I don't have order exports yet. Walk me through what to pull and give empty templates.
evals.expected_output: Export checklist; formulas; template bundle cards with placeholders; hypothetical example clearly labeled; no fake store metrics as real.
expectations: Explains minimum data fields needed from order history for co-purchase analysis
Includes template bundle cards with Hero, Accessory A, Accessory B, Bundle discount price, and One-click checkout hook placeholders
Includes empty or illustrative topology table structure
Does not present fabricated order counts as actual user data

evals.prompt: Translate our refund policy into French. No bundles.
evals.expected_output: Translation or policy help; does not force co-purchase bundle cards.
expectations: Does not output the full mandatory bundle recommendation card structure as the main deliverable
Does not force association coefficients or FBT logic for a pure translation request
